theme: warehouse clerk stacking crates that leave on schedule
title: Loading-Dock Shelf: Reuse Under a Known Departure Schedule
strategies: bump allocator: give every crate its own never-reused shelf offset in id order (the checker's own baseline, ~0.1)

unified best-fit sweep (offline dynamic storage allocation): process births/deaths in time order, always reuse the smallest free hole that fits, breaking ties by lowest address -- shrinks the footprint but is blind to which crates get checked often, so it scatters hot crates across whichever hole happened to be free

hotness-bucketed best-fit: split the free-hole pool into a 'hot' bucket (holes vacated by, and preferentially reused by, frequently-checked crates) and a 'cold' bucket for everything else, computed from the checked-crate trace, and only fall back to the other bucket or a fresh bump region when the preferred bucket has no fit -- trades a little footprint for far fewer distinct aisles touched
further metaheuristics: simulated annealing / exchange search over hole assignment jointly optimizing footprint and aisle-touch count within the time budget
